citations.citation_title: Clinical Practice Guidelines Linked to the International Classification of Functioning, Disability and Health
citations.guideline_source: American Physical Therapy Association (APTA) Academy of Orthopaedic Physical Therapy - Clinical Practice Guidelines (ongoing series, e.g., Neck Pain 2017, Low Back Pain 2021, Shoulder Pain 2013)
citations.quote: APTA clinical practice guidelines recommend that physical therapy duration and frequency be individualized based on patient response, with ongoing reassessment of validated outcome measures to guide continuation versus discharge rather than fixed visit limits.
citations.supports_claim: Physical therapy duration should be determined by documented functional progress on validated outcome measures, not by arbitrary visit caps, supporting extension when measurable improvement is ongoing.

citations.quote: The Guide to Physical Therapist Practice directs clinicians to use standardized outcome measures at regular intervals to evaluate progress toward goals and to modify or continue the plan of care as long as the patient is demonstrating meaningful functional improvement.
citations.supports_claim: Continued skilled PT is indicated when serial objective outcome measures demonstrate measurable functional improvement toward plan-of-care goals.

citations.citation_title: Noninvasive Treatments for Acute, Subacute, and Chronic Low Back Pain: Clinical Practice Guideline
citations.quote: ACP recommends non-pharmacologic therapies including physical therapy, exercise, and multidisciplinary rehabilitation as first-line treatment for chronic low back pain, with continuation based on clinical response.
citations.supports_claim: Guideline-endorsed first-line conservative therapy (physical therapy) should be continued when the patient is responding, supporting extension of authorized visits.

source_quotes.quote: Your request for an additional 12 physical therapy visits for post-operative rotator cuff repair rehabilitation has been DENIED.

Reason for denial: Member has exhausted the standard benefit allowance of 20 visits per episode of care. Request does not meet criteria for extension per policy REHAB-PT-v5, which requires documentation of ongoing measurable functional progress.
You have the right to appeal within 60 days.

citations.claim: The denial was issued for an additional 12 physical therapy visits for post-operative rotator cuff repair rehabilitation.
citations.verbatim_quote: Your request for an additional 12 physical therapy visits for post-operative rotator cuff repair rehabilitation has been DENIED.

citations.claim: The stated reasons were exhaustion of the 20-visit benefit and failure to meet REHAB-PT-v5 extension criteria.
citations.verbatim_quote: Reason for denial: Member has exhausted the standard benefit allowance of 20 visits per episode of care. Request does not meet criteria for extension per policy REHAB-PT-v5, which requires documentation of ongoing measurable functional progress.

citations.claim: The denial code assigned is REHAB-MAX-01.
The member has appeal rights within 60 days of the denial.
citations.verbatim_quote: You have the right to appeal within 60 days.

paragraphs.heading: Patient and Requested Service
paragraphs.text: This letter formally appeals Meridian Health Plus's denial dated April 5, 2026 regarding our member, Maria S. Gonzalez (Member ID MH-44219087, DOB August 22, 1967). The denial states that the request for an additional 12 physical therapy visits for post-operative rotator cuff repair rehabilitation has been denied under denial code REHAB-MAX-01, on the grounds that the standard 20-visit benefit has been exhausted and that the extension criteria of policy REHAB-PT-v5 are not met. As the treating surgeon, I respectfully disagree: the medical record demonstrates that Ms. Gonzalez meets every element of the extension criteria set out in REHAB-PT-v5, and I submit this appeal within the 60-day appeal window.

citations.claim: REHAB-PT-v5 requires that initial plan-of-care functional goals have not yet been achieved.
citations.verbatim_quote: Member has not yet achieved functional goals stated in the initial plan of care.

citations.claim: The initial plan of care established functional goals of flexion 150 degrees, abduction 140 degrees, and return to work as a bookkeeper requiring overhead reaching.
citations.verbatim_quote: Goals: functional flexion 150 deg, abduction 140 deg, return to work (bookkeeper, requires reaching overhead for files) by week 16.

citations.claim: The surgeon documents that Ms. Gonzalez is not yet at the goal range of 150/140 flexion/abduction but is on track.
citations.verbatim_quote: Not yet at full functional range (goal 150/140 flexion/abduction) but on track.

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Initial plan-of-care functional goals not yet achieved
paragraphs.text: Policy REHAB-PT-v5 requires that the member has not yet achieved the functional goals stated in the initial plan of care. The initial PT evaluation on 2026-02-19 established objective functional goals of flexion 150 degrees, abduction 140 degrees, and return to work as a bookkeeper (a role requiring overhead reaching for files) by week 16. As documented in the surgeon progress note of 2026-04-03, Ms. Gonzalez is currently at 130 degrees of flexion and 115 degrees of abduction — improving steadily, but not yet at the goals. This criterion is clearly met.

citations.claim: The policy requires measurable improvement in at least two objective measures over the most recent four weeks.
citations.verbatim_quote:
Documentation shows measurable functional improvement in at least two objective measures (range of
motion, strength, functional outcome score such as DASH, Oswestry, LEFS, etc.) over the most recent four
weeks.

citations.claim: The most recent four-week block (visits 11-20) documents measurable improvement in flexion, abduction, external rotation, pain, and DASH score.
citations.verbatim_quote: Flexion: 105 -> 130 deg. Abduction: 90 -> 115 deg. ER: 30 -> 45 deg. Pain with activity: 4/10 -> 2/10. DASH: 54 -> 38. Gains continue with each session.

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Measurable functional improvement on multiple objective measures over the most recent four weeks
paragraphs.text: The policy requires documented measurable functional improvement in at least two objective measures (e.g., range of motion, strength, or a validated outcome score such as DASH) over the most recent four weeks. The PT progress summary for visits 11–20, through 2026-04-02, documents improvement across four objective measures in the most recent four-week block: flexion improved from 105 to 130 degrees, abduction from 90 to 115 degrees, external rotation from 30 to 45 degrees, pain with activity decreased from 4/10 to 2/10, and the DASH functional outcome score improved from 54 to 38. These gains — on four separate objective measures, including a validated outcome score explicitly named in the policy — decisively exceed the two-measure threshold. This pattern is consistent with APTA Guide to Physical Therapist Practice 3.0 and APTA Clinical Practice Guidelines, which direct clinicians to use serial standardized outcome measures to guide continuation of care when the patient is demonstrating meaningful functional improvement.

citations.claim: The policy requires a written statement from the therapist and/or physician that additional visits will produce further measurable improvement within a specific number of sessions.
citations.verbatim_quote:
Treating therapist and/or physician provide a written statement that additional visits are expected to result in
further measurable improvement within a specific number of additional sessions.

citations.claim: The treating surgeon documents that approximately 8-12 additional sessions are required to achieve work-readiness.
citations.verbatim_quote: Requires approximately 8-12 additional sessions to achieve work-readiness for her bookkeeping role (overhead reaching requirement).

citations.claim: The PT plan of care specifies objective targets achievable within 8-12 additional sessions based on the trajectory of the prior 20 sessions.
citations.verbatim_quote: Request 12 additional visits. Current objective goals: flexion 150, abduction 140, ER 60, DASH <= 20. Based on trajectory of prior 20 sessions, these goals are achievable within 8-12 sessions.

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Written statement that additional visits will produce further measurable improvement within a specific number of sessions
paragraphs.text: The policy requires that the treating therapist and/or physician provide a written statement that additional visits are expected to result in further measurable improvement within a specific number of additional sessions. Both the treating physical therapist and I have provided such statements. The surgeon progress note of 2026-04-03 states that Ms. Gonzalez requires approximately 8–12 additional sessions to achieve work-readiness for her bookkeeping role. The PT plan of care of 2026-04-03 similarly requests 12 additional visits with specified objective targets (flexion 150, abduction 140, ER 60, DASH ≤ 20) and states that, based on the trajectory of the prior 20 sessions, these goals are achievable within 8–12 sessions. This criterion is met in writing by both providers.

citations.claim: The policy defines plateau as no measurable improvement in objective metrics over four consecutive weeks.
citations.verbatim_quote:
Member's progress is not plateaued. A plateau is defined as no measurable improvement in objective metrics
over four consecutive weeks.

citations.claim: PT documentation states that gains continue with each session.
The treating surgeon independently documents continuing week-over-week functional gains.
citations.verbatim_quote: Continuing to demonstrate week-over-week functional gains.

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Progress is not plateaued
paragraphs.text: The policy requires that the member's progress not be plateaued, with plateau defined as no measurable improvement in objective metrics over four consecutive weeks. The chart demonstrates the opposite pattern. The PT progress summary for visits 11–20 explicitly states that gains continue with each session, and the treating surgeon independently documents continuing week-over-week functional gains as of 2026-04-03. There has been no four-week interval of stagnation; gains have been sustained and documented across every reassessment period. Accordingly, the plateau exclusion does not apply.

paragraphs.heading: Conclusion and Request
paragraphs.text: Ms. Gonzalez meets every one of the four extension criteria in REHAB-PT-v5: (1) she has not yet achieved her initial plan-of-care goals for flexion, abduction, and return to work; (2) documentation over the most recent four weeks shows measurable improvement in four objective measures, including the DASH functional outcome score explicitly named in the policy; (3) both the treating surgeon and the treating therapist have provided written statements that 8–12 additional visits are expected to produce further measurable improvement toward defined objective targets; and (4) her progress is not plateaued — week-over-week gains are documented by both providers. Discontinuing skilled physical therapy at this point, when the patient is actively improving and within a small number of sessions of her functional and return-to-work goals, is not clinically appropriate and is inconsistent with the plan's own extension policy as well as APTA clinical practice guidance. I respectfully request that Meridian Health Plus overturn the denial and authorize the requested 12 additional physical therapy visits. Thank you for your prompt reconsideration.

source_quotes.quote:
EXTENSION CRITERIA: Additional visits (up to 20 more) may be approved when ALL of the following are
documented:
Member has not yet achieved functional goals stated in the initial plan of care.

Documentation shows measurable functional improvement in at least two objective measures (range of
motion, strength, functional outcome score such as DASH, Oswestry, LEFS, etc.) over the most recent four
weeks.

Treating therapist and/or physician provide a written statement that additional visits are expected to result in
further measurable improvement within a specific number of additional sessions.

Member's progress is not plateaued. A plateau is defined as no measurable improvement in objective metrics
over four consecutive weeks.
